general services officer with the complete description and cost segregation of
road components for road projects; and

unrecorded Road Networks like reconciling the accounting records with the
roadmap. For the amount to be transferred to the accounting records, the

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (a and b) instruct the General Services Officer-Designate to maintain an efficient property management by following the prescribed procedures in property recording and maintain accurate property records; (c) regularly renew Property Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR); (d) require clearance from retired, resigned, or separated from the service officials and employees to clear them of their property accountability; and (b) the Accounting Office to maintain an updated property, plant and equipment ledger cards reconciled with the property cards of the GSO.

We recommended and Management agreed that the required physical inventory be conducted regularly for the Inventory account and prepare a report thereon to come up with a correct and reliable figure in the financial statement. Further, submit to the Accounting Office the Requisition and Issue Slip supported with acknowledgement Receipt from end users and recipients for immediate recording in the books.

We recommended that the required supporting documents as per COA Circular 2012-001 are attached for every cash advances made to ascertain the propriety and correctness of cash advances made and to avoid the incurrence of excess cash advances.
It is earnestly recommended for the management to insure their properties to be indemnified in case of damages caused by fortuitous events.

We recommended that the GSO-designate prepare and submit monthly the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued to the Accounting Office for proper accounting and recording in the books to present fairly the monthly financial statements of the agency. Further, instruct the personnel or requisitioning unit concerned to accomplish a Requisition and Issue Slip when requesting supplies and materials.
We recommended and management committed to follow the provisions of the CSC and DBM Joint Circular on overtime pay to avoid the incurrence of expenses that could be disallowed in audit.
We recommended that Management strictly comply with the NGAS accounting policy on the recording of all types of inventories to strengthen control over their assets.

We recommended that management strictly comply with the pertinent provisions of the revised IRR of RA No. 9184 especially the consolidation of their PPMP. Require the School Principals to prepare ahead their Individual PPMP for consolidation to avoid procurement on a piecemeal basis and ensure transparency and be able to obtain the most advantageous price for the government.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to record only obligations which are valid claims duly supported with documents in accordance with Sec 157 of GAAM Vol. I. Require the concerned departments to prepare the supporting documents for the recorded obligations and make the necessary adjusting entry for the claims not duly supported to present fairly the financial statements.
We recommended that Management adhere strictly to the provisions of Local Budget Circular No. 103 and COA Circular 2012-003 to avoid incurrence of additional expenses.

We  recommended  that lump-sum appropriations under the Annual Investment Plan be supported with detailed/specific programs, projects and activities as required under the Local Government Code for efficient planning. Further, if the project is described in generic terms, a prior approval or authorization of the sanggunian is required in accordance with Sec 22c of RA 7160.

We recommended and Management agreed that only the allowed expenditures be charged against the Special Education Fund in accordance with DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1 s. 2017 so that funds may not be depleted for its intended purpose.
We recommend that management strictly adhere with the provisions of RA 9184 to attain the most advantageous price for all procurement projects of the municipality.

We recommended that Management require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to strictly comply with the provisions of Section 54.1 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184. Also, copy of NOA and NTP posted to PhilGEPS should be attached to the disbursement voucher.

We recommended that Management require the PMC to strictly monitor all infrastructure projects implemented and prepare and update the required report to contribute to the prompt completion and delivery of services to its constituents. Compute and cause the payment of liquidated damages applicable to the project.

We recommended and Management agreed to refrain from paying registration fees directly to the hotel venue and require official receipts from the sponsoring agency or group for complete documentation of government expenditures.

We recommended that Department Heads require the preparation/submission of the DTR and for the Municipal Accountant to ensure that payments will be made only if the claims are properly and completely documented to avoid possible suspensions and disallowances in audit.

We recommended and Management agreed to appropriate funds to cover the necessary benefits of the employees authorized by law.
We recommended that Management should not peg the Subsistence and Laundry Allowance to P900.00 and P125.00, respectively, but rather compute the allowance on a per day basis as required under existing rules and regulations. We likewise, recommend that an employee must have a written authority from the LCE before he/she could avail of subsistence allowance for “on-call” days. We further recommend that the agency should implement a stronger control in its payroll system in order to avoid situations where employees are paid for days that they are not allowed to receive Subsistence Allowance.
We recommended that the concerned employees refund the total amount of surcharges paid pursuant to Sec 2 of PD 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Agriculture Office carefully evaluate the recipients of projects under RA 7171 so that the intended support is accorded to the legitimate tobacco farmers in accordance with issued guidelines. Also, orient the recipient on the terms and conditions of the Memorandum of Agreement particularly the maintenance of the equipment to avoid wastage of government funds.

We recommended the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to include only in the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Plan projects and activities that are in line with the JMC 2013-01 and charge to the General Fund expenses that are general in nature so that funds to address problems arising from calamities are readily available for its purpose.
We recommended that Management adhere to the policies and guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.

We have recommended that management instruct the GSO-Designate to prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report for Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP). Determine also if the unserviceable and obsolete properties are valueless or unusable.  If so, these properties be condemned and the corresponding value be dropped from the books in order to reflect the correct value of Property, Plant and Equipment in the financial statements.
